records.role: Vice President of the United States
Secretary of Health and Human Services
Secretary of Housing and Urban Development
Director of the Office of Management and Budget

records.note: Assumed the role in an acting capacity 2025-05-01 after Mike Waltz was removed; concurrently remains Secretary of State (first dual-hat since Kissinger). Still acting NSA as of mid-2026 per NPR (2026-03-14); no permanent NSA named.
Original second-term NSA; removed 2025-05-01 in the first major shakeup (amid 'Signalgate') and subsequently moved toward the UN Ambassador post. Included as the notable predecessor.

Longtime Rubio aide promoted from State Dept (Counselor / Director of Policy Planning) to deputy NSA on 2026-05-26, described by WaPo/Axios as the 'top White House national security post.' Serves alongside other deputy NSAs Andy Baker and Robert Gabriel Jr.; no source uses the literal 'Principal Deputy' title.
Named deputy NSA in the May 2025 NSC restructuring; concurrently serves as VP JD Vance's national security advisor (since Jan 2025). Included as a co-deputy NSA.
Holds the Homeland Security Advisor title concurrently with White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy since inauguration (same person as wh-deputy-cos-policy).

records.role: Director of the National Economic Council
records.note: Still NEC director as of mid-2026. Considered for Fed Chair but Trump nominated Kevin Warsh (Jan 2026) instead, explicitly to keep Hassett at the NEC.

records.role: Acting Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers
records.note: CEA Vice Chairman (Feb 2025) who became Acting Chair when Stephen Miran took leave for the Fed Board; remains acting chair pending Senate action on nominee Christopher Phelan.

records.role: Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers (nominee)
records.note: University of Minnesota economist nominated 2026-04-21 to chair CEA; Senate Banking Committee confirmation hearing held 2026-06-18. Not yet confirmed as of as_of date.

records.role: Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers
records.note: First Trump-2 CEA chair (confirmed mid-March 2025; start date approximate). Took leave Sept 2025 for an interim Federal Reserve Board seat, then formally resigned the CEA chair 2026-02-03 to remain at the Fed. Notable predecessor.

records.role: Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy
records.note: Senate-confirmed OSTP director (74-25) on 2025-03-25; still serving in 2026.

records.role: Chair of the Council on Environmental Quality
records.note: 13th CEQ chair; acting from 2025-01-20, Senate-confirmed mid-September 2025.

records.role: White House AI and Crypto Czar (Special Advisor for AI and Crypto)
records.note: Left the czar role late March 2026 after exhausting his 130-day special-government-employee limit, moving to co-chair PCAST. No successor named; the AI and Crypto Czar position is vacant as of the as_of date.

records.role: Counsel to the President (White House Counsel)
White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy
records.note: Concurrently serves as Homeland Security Advisor (see wh-homeland-security-advisor).

records.role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Operations
records.note: William 'Beau' Harrison; serving since Jan 2025 through mid-2026.

records.role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Legislative, Political and Public Affairs (Acting)
records.note: Former RNC chief of staff who joined the White House ~2026-05-01 to fill this role while James Blair is on leave running Trump's 2026 midterm operation; Wikipedia/LegiStorm describe him as acting.

records.role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Legislative, Political and Public Affairs
records.note: Took a leave of absence (~April 2026) to run Trump's 2026 midterm political operation; retains an Assistant to the President title while Richard Walters fills the role in an acting capacity. Some June 2026 sources still attribute the title to Blair, so 'former' reflects his stepping away from active duties rather than a clean departure.

records.role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Strategic Implementation
records.note: Still holds the role as of late June 2026, but reported (June 2026) to be slated to become VP JD Vance's chief of staff around August 2026 — record may go stale shortly after the as_of date.

Held the dual title 'Deputy Chief of Staff for Communications and Personnel, and Cabinet Secretary' from inauguration; departed by 2025-10-01 (announced 2025-09-24, Axios). No clearly named successor as Cabinet Secretary was found and the related Director of Cabinet Affairs (Lea Bardon) also left ~April 2026, so the Cabinet Secretary role appears vacant/absorbed as of the as_of date — included only as the sourced former holder.

records.role: Director of the Office of National Drug Control Policy (Drug Czar)
records.note: Nominated March 2025; Senate-confirmed 52-48 on 2026-01-06 as the 10th ONDCP Director and first woman to lead the office.

records.role: Director of the Office of Presidential Personnel
records.note: Took over PPO on 2025-10-13 after Sergio Gor was confirmed Ambassador to India; concurrently remains a White House Deputy Chief of Staff (Assistant to the President) since 2025-01-20.

records.role: Director of the Office of Presidential Personnel
records.note: Led PPO from inauguration; nominated U.S. Ambassador to India (Aug 2025) and confirmed, ending PPO tenure (~Oct 2025); serving as Ambassador to India since 2026.

records.role: Director of the Domestic Policy Council
records.note: DPC Director since inauguration; still serving in 2026.

records.role: Director of the White House Office of Public Liaison
records.note: Deputy Assistant to the President and Director of the Office of Public Liaison since Jan 2025; still listed as current in 2026 (LegiStorm; Punchbowl 2026 Power List).

records.role: Senior Counselor to the President for Trade and Manufacturing
records.note: Assistant to the President and Senior Counselor for Trade and Manufacturing; in office since inauguration.

records.role: Senior Advisor to the President (de facto head of DOGE)
records.note: Served as Senior Advisor and de facto DOGE head as a special government employee; departed late May 2025 when the 130-day SGE limit expired (sources vary May 28 vs May 30, 2025). Included as a notable former senior advisor.

Confirmed/sworn DAG March 2025 and still nominally holds the title (no successor DAG named). Became Acting Attorney General on 2026-04-02 after Trump fired Pam Bondi; nominated for permanent AG in June 2026.
Confirmed Oct 2025; assumed office 2025-11-14. April 5, 2026 reports of a possible Dhillon promotion / Woodward demotion remain unconfirmed.
Confirmed by the Senate 52-45 on 2025-04-04, took office same day.
9th FBI Director; sworn in 2025-02-20; confirmed serving as of June 2026.
Sworn in July 2025 after Senate confirmation; still serving (DEA statement dated 2026-06-25).

records.jurisdiction: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives
records.note: Career ATF agent; Senate-confirmed ~2026-04-29 and sworn in 2026-04-30; previously ATF Deputy Director since April 2025.
13th USMS Director; Senate-confirmed 2025-07-31, sworn in 2025-08-01. Career law-enforcement official.

records.role: Director of the Bureau of Prisons (DOJ)
records.note: Appointed by President Trump and sworn in 2025-04-21; former West Virginia corrections commissioner. BOP Director is not a Senate-confirmed position.

Michael Faulkender (16th Deputy Secretary, confirmed 2025-03-26) resigned 2025-08-22. Treasury announced (SB-0479, 2026-04-29) Brooke would perform the delegable duties of Deputy Secretary; also nominated to the post 2026-05-11 (pending). Concurrently Acting Under Secretary for International Affairs.

Office churned through several acting heads in 2025 (Gary Shapley, Michael Faulkender ~Apr 2025); Billy Long sworn in 2025-06-16 as 51st Commissioner, removed 2025-08-08, after which Treasury Secretary Bessent took over as acting. IRS.gov still lists him as 'Commissioner (Acting)' mid-2026; Senate Finance Democrats argue his statutory acting term expired 2026-03-06.
Career/non-partisan official; assumed office September 2023, retained under the Trump administration. Still in role per HSGAC Senate letter dated 2026-04-17.
46th Treasurer; former Georgia (Republican) state senator; named by Trump 2025-03-23. Position no longer requires Senate confirmation. Start date varies by source.

records.jurisdiction: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
records.note: Sworn in as the 32nd Comptroller of the Currency on 2025-07-15.

records.role: Under Secretary for Terrorism and Financial Intelligence (Treasury)
records.note: Confirmed by the Senate 51-47 in July 2025 (Treasury announcement dated 2025-07-23), succeeding acting head Brad Smith.
Confirmed by the Senate 2025-03-24; took office 2025-03-25. Principal Deputy Secretary (the No. 2 official at State).

records.role: Deputy Secretary of State for Management and Resources
records.note: Second deputy secretary position. SFRC advanced nomination 16-6 on 2025-03-12; assumed office 2025-05-19.

records.role: U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations
records.note: 32nd U.S. Permanent Representative to the UN. Confirmed 47-43 on 2025-09-19; sworn in 2025-09-20. Previously Trump's National Security Advisor.

records.jurisdiction: U.S. Agency for International Development (functions merged into U.S. Department of State)
records.note: USAID was dismantled in 2025: Secretary of State Rubio was designated Acting Administrator in early Feb 2025 and oversaw the wind-down. USAID officially ceased implementing foreign assistance 2025-07-01, with remaining functions absorbed into the State Department (NPR, 2025-07-01). No Senate-confirmed USAID Administrator was installed.
Cerberus co-founder; confirmed 59-40 on 2025-03-14, sworn in 2025-03-17 as 36th Deputy Secretary of Defense.
26th Secretary of the Army; confirmed and sworn in 2025-02-25. Reported clashes with SecDef Hegseth (Washington Post, 2026-04) but remains in office as of June 2026.
Under Secretary of the Navy (sworn 2025-10-03) elevated to acting SecNav after SecDef Hegseth removed John Phelan (79th SecNav) on 2026-04-22 (Military Times/CNN/Al Jazeera 2026-04-22/23).
27th Secretary of the Air Force (also oversees the Space Force); former Principal Deputy Director of the NRO. Start date approximate (confirmed/sworn May 2025).

records.role: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
records.note: 21st CJCS; recalled from retirement after Trump fired Gen. C.Q. Brown Jr. in Feb 2025. Sworn in 2025-04-11.

records.role: Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
records.note: 13th VCJCS (USMC); confirmed 2025-09-19, sworn in 2025-10-01, succeeding Adm. Christopher Grady.

records.role: Acting Chief of Staff of the Army
records.note: Vice Chief of Staff of the Army (40th, since 2026-02-06) became acting CSA after SecDef Hegseth removed Gen. Randy George (41st CSA) on 2026-04-02. No Senate-confirmed permanent successor reported as of June 2026.
34th CNO, sworn in 2025-08-25; followed Adm. Lisa Franchetti's removal (Feb 2025) and Adm. James Kilby's acting tenure.

records.role: Chief of Staff of the Air Force
records.note: 24th CSAF; confirmed 2025-10-30, sworn in 2025-11-03, succeeding the retiring Gen. David Allvin.
2nd Chief of Space Operations, in office since 2022-11-02; still serving per April 2026 Space Symposium and official bio.
39th Commandant of the Marine Corps, in office since 2023-09-22; still serving as of June 2026.

Sworn in as 9th Deputy Secretary on 2025-03-08; nomination to be Ambassador to El Salvador withdrawn 2026-04-13, remained Deputy Secretary. Serves under Secretary Markwayne Mullin (confirmed 2026-03-24).
U.S. Senate confirmed 2025-06-18; sworn in shortly after.

Acting director since ~2026-06-01, succeeding Todd Lyons (Mar 2025-May 2026). On 2026-06-27 Trump named Lance Schroyer as nominee for permanent ICE Director. ICE has had no Senate-confirmed director since 2017.
Senate-confirmed; began service as Director on 2025-07-15.

records.role: TSA Administrator, Acting (Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator) (DHS)
records.note: Named Deputy Administrator April 2025; serving as Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator through at least mid-2026. Trump nominated David Cummins as permanent TSA Administrator (~2026-05-11), pending Senate confirmation.

records.role: FEMA Administrator, Acting (Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator) (DHS)
records.note: Career FEMA official (Region 9 Administrator since 2015) serving as acting head from ~May 2026 amid repeated 2025-2026 leadership turnover (Cameron Hamilton, David Richardson, et al.). Trump nominated Cameron Hamilton (~2026-05-11) as permanent Administrator. FEMA has had no Senate-confirmed administrator under the second Trump term.

Executive Assistant Director for Cybersecurity serving as acting CISA Director since ~2026-02-26, after Madhu Gottumukkala was reassigned within DHS. Nominee Sean Plankey withdrew in April 2026; no Senate-confirmed director.
28th Director; the Secret Service Director is a presidential appointment that does not require Senate confirmation.
Uniformed officer (Admiral). Served as Acting Commandant 2025-01-21 to 2026-01-15, then sworn in as the 28th Commandant on 2026-01-15.

records.role: Deputy Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS)
records.jurisdiction: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
records.note: Nominated by Trump on 2026-06-25; not yet Senate-confirmed. Office vacant since predecessor Jim O'Neill (Deputy Secretary June 2025-Feb 2026) departed ~2026-02-13 to lead NSF. Klomp currently serves as HHS chief counselor and head of Medicare.

records.jurisdiction: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
records.note: Serving as Acting CDC Director concurrently with his role as NIH Director. Predecessor Susan Monarez (confirmed July 2025) was ousted 2025-08-27; Jim O'Neill acted Aug 2025-Feb 2026 before leaving for NSF.

records.role: Acting Commissioner of Food and Drugs / FDA Commissioner (HHS)
records.note: Elevated from FDA Deputy Commissioner for Food to Acting Commissioner after Marty Makary resigned/was forced out ~2026-05-12. No permanent nominee confirmed as of 2026-06-29 (Heidi Overton reported as a leading candidate).
18th NIH Director; Senate-confirmed 2025-03-25, took office 2025-04-01. Also serving as Acting CDC Director since 2026-02-18.

records.role: Administrator, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HHS)
records.note: 17th CMS Administrator; Senate-confirmed 2025-04-03 (53-45), assumed office 2025-04-08.

records.role: Acting Surgeon General of the United States (HHS)
records.jurisdiction: Office of the Surgeon General, U.S. Public Health Service
records.note: OSG senior adviser carrying out Surgeon General duties on an interim basis (announced ~2026-05-19/20). Pending Senate nominee is Nicole Saphier (nominated 2026-04-30). Prior nominees withdrawn: Casey Means (2026-04-30) and Janette Nesheiwat (2025).

records.role: NOAA Administrator / Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere (Commerce)
records.note: Nominated Feb 2025; Senate-confirmed early Oct 2025 (reported Oct 7-8, 2025). Previously acting NOAA administrator in first Trump term.

records.role: Performing the Duties of the Director, U.S. Census Bureau (Commerce)
records.note: Title is 'Performing the Duties of Director' (acting); no Senate-confirmed director. Announced via Sept 19, 2025 memo, taking over from acting director Ron Jarmin (who followed the Feb 2025 departure of director Robert Santos).

records.role: NIST Director / Under Secretary of Commerce for Standards and Technology (Commerce)
records.jurisdiction: National Institute of Standards and Technology
records.note: Nominated Oct 2025; Senate-confirmed 46-45 on 2026-05-19. 18th NIST Director; former dean of engineering at Purdue University.

records.role: USPTO Director / Under Secretary of Commerce for Intellectual Property (Commerce)
records.note: Nominated March 2025; Senate-confirmed 51-47 on 2025-09-18 (en bloc); USPTO welcome announcement dated 2025-09-22.

Career NPS official (former comptroller); acting director since 2025-01-20. Trump nominee Scott Socha (nominated 2026-02-11) was withdrawn 2026-04-28, so no Senate-confirmed director is in place as of 2026-06-29.
Former NM GOP congressman and state party chair; Senate-confirmed 46-43 on 2026-05-18, sworn in 2026-05-19 as the 20th BLM Director. Sometimes formally listed as 'Stevan Pearce.'
Senate-confirmed 51-47 on 2025-10-07; assumed office 2025-11-18 as the 19th USGS Director (succeeding David Applegate).

records.role: Chief of the U.S. Forest Service (USDA)
records.note: 21st Chief; named by Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins on 2025-02-27 and sworn in 2025-03-10, succeeding retiring Chief Randy Moore. Non-PAS agency-head post; first chief appointed from outside the agency.

records.role: Acting Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (Labor)
records.note: Career Deputy Commissioner serving as Acting Commissioner since early Aug 2025, after Trump fired confirmed Commissioner Erika McEntarfer on 2025-08-01. First replacement pick E.J. Antoni (nominated ~2025-08-11) was withdrawn ~Sept 2025.

records.role: Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (Labor) — nominee
records.note: Career BLS economist nominated by Trump in late Jan 2026 after the E.J. Antoni nomination was withdrawn. Senate HELP Committee vote set for 2026-06-24; no source confirms full Senate confirmation as of 2026-06-29.

records.role: NNSA Administrator / Under Secretary for Nuclear Security (DOE)
records.note: Former U.S. Rep. (NY-22); Senate-confirmed 2025-09-18 in a party-line block vote. The Under Secretary for Nuclear Security also serves as NNSA Administrator.
Former Republic Airways CEO; Senate-confirmed 53-43 on 2025-07-09; FAA welcomed him as Administrator 2025-07-10.
Former NHTSA Chief Counsel (2017-2021); Senate-confirmed on 2025-09-18. Peter Simshauser had been Acting Administrator prior.

records.role: Under Secretary of Education (also performing delegated duties of Deputy Secretary)
records.note: No Senate-confirmed Deputy Secretary of Education (Penny Schwinn's nomination withdrawn 2025-09-03). Kent confirmed as Under Secretary 50-45 on 2025-08-01, sworn in 2025-08-04, holds delegated authority to perform Deputy Secretary functions. A March 2025 EO targeted the Department for dismantlement, but Congress has not abolished it.

11th Deputy Secretary of VA; Senate-confirmed 2025-03-27, sworn in 2025-03-28 by Secretary Doug Collins. As of early 2026 also performing the delegable duties of Assistant Secretary for Information and Technology / CIO.

Transition: Jerome H. Powell's term as Chair ended; the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh 54-45 on 2026-05-13 (NPR/CNBC) and he took the oath as Chair on 2026-05-22 (federalreserve.gov). Powell remains on the Board as a governor. Warsh is the 17th Fed Chair; four-year term runs to 2030.

records.role: Vice Chair of the Federal Reserve
records.note: Took office as Vice Chair on 2023-09-13 for a four-year term ending 2027-09-07; still serving as of 2026-06-29.

records.role: Vice Chair for Supervision of the Federal Reserve
Chairman of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
Chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission
records.note: Nominated 2025-10-27; confirmed by the Senate 2025-12-18; sworn in as 16th CFTC Chairman on 2025-12-22. Previously chief counsel of the SEC's Crypto Task Force. Caroline Pham had been acting chair earlier in 2025.

records.role: Chairman of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
records.note: Confirmed by the Senate 2025-12-18; sworn in as 23rd FDIC Chairman on 2026-01-13 for a five-year term. Had served as Acting Chairman from 2025-01-20 until confirmation.

records.role: Chairman of the National Credit Union Administration
records.note: Designated NCUA Chairman by President Trump in January 2025. His six-year board term expired in August 2025, but he is serving in holdover capacity as Chairman pending a confirmed successor (John Crews nominated). Two NCUA board seats remain vacant amid litigation over the April 2025 dismissals of Todd Harper and Tanya Otsuka.

records.role: Acting Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
records.note: Contested leadership. Russell Vought (also OMB Director) has been CFPB Acting Director since Feb. 2025 and remained acting as of late June 2026, but federal law bars him from serving past 2026-08-01. Per a 2026-06-02 report, Chief Legal Officer Mark Paoletta was elevated to Deputy Director and is slated to become Acting Director on Vought's exit. Separately, on 2026-06-10 President Trump nominated Brian Johnson as permanent CFPB Director (his third nominee, after Jonathan McKernan and Stuart Levenbach).

records.role: Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency
records.note: Senate-confirmed (March 2025); sworn in 2025-03-14 as 5th FHFA Director. In June 2026 Pulte was also named Acting Director of National Intelligence while retaining his FHFA directorship and chairmanship of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.

records.role: Chair of the Federal Trade Commission
Chairman of the Federal Communications Commission
Chairman of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
records.note: Confirmed as Commissioner 2025-10-07 (term to 2030-06-30); designated Chairman 2025-10-23, succeeding acting chair David Rosner. Republican Mark Christie chaired FERC earlier in 2025 before departing.

records.role: Chairman of the National Labor Relations Board
records.note: After 2025 NLRB quorum/membership turmoil, Murphy was sworn in as a Board Member 2026-01-07 (term to 2027-12-16) and designated Chairman by President Trump on 2026-03-27, succeeding former Chairman Marvin Kaplan, to whom Murphy had been Chief Counsel.

records.role: Chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
records.note: Lucas (Commissioner since 2020) was named Acting Chair on 2025-01-20, reconfirmed by the Senate 2025-07-31 for a term to 2030-07-01, and formally designated Chair (announced 2025-11-06).

records.role: President and CEO of the Securities Investor Protection Corporation
records.note: Post became President and CEO on 2025-07-01, succeeding Josephine Wang, who retired 2025-06-30 after 41 years.

records.role: Chairman of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board
records.note: Appointed by the SEC (announced 2026-01-30) and sworn in 2026-02-10 amid the 2025-2026 PCAOB leadership overhaul; term expires 2030-10-24. Sworn in alongside new board members Mark Calabria and Steven Laughton.

records.role: President and Chairman of the Export-Import Bank of the United States
records.jurisdiction: Export-Import Bank of the United States
records.note: Confirmed by the U.S. Senate as President and Chairman (announced 2025-09-19); term expiring 2029-01-20.

records.role: Administrator of the Small Business Administration
records.note: 28th SBA Administrator; former U.S. Senator from Georgia. Confirmed by the Senate 2025-02-19.

Trump nominated Isaacman in late 2024/early 2025; nomination withdrawn May 2025; re-nominated 2025-11-04. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy served as acting/interim administrator from July 2025 until Isaacman was confirmed by the Senate 67-30 on 2025-12-17 and sworn in (15th Administrator) 2025-12-18.

records.role: Chairman of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission
records.note: Nieh sworn in as NRC Commissioner 2025-12-04 (term ending 2029-06-30) and designated 20th Chairman by President Trump on 2026-01-08, replacing David Wright (designated chair 2025-01-21, remains a commissioner).
Senate confirmed Bisignano 53-47 on 2025-05-06; sworn in as the 18th Senate-confirmed SSA Commissioner in May 2025 (SSA marked his one-year anniversary on 2026-05-07).

records.role: Administrator of the General Services Administration
records.note: Senate confirmed Forst 2025-12-18; sworn in as GSA Administrator 2025-12-24. Stephen Ehikian had served as acting administrator earlier in 2025.

records.role: Director of the Office of Personnel Management
records.note: Senate confirmed Kupor 49-46 on 2025-07-09; sworn in as OPM Director 2025-07-14.

records.role: Acting Director of the National Science Foundation
records.note: Director Sethuraman Panchanathan resigned effective 2025-04-24; chief of staff Brian Stone has performed the duties of director since. President Trump nominated Jim (James) O'Neill as NSF Director on 2026-03-02; as of late June 2026 the nomination was still pending before the Senate HELP Committee, so Stone remains acting director.

records.role: Chair of the Federal Election Commission
records.note: FEC chair rotates annually. Broussard (Trump-nominated 2020) was elected Chair effective 2025-07-01 (Campaigns & Elections), her second time as chair (also 2021), succeeding Ellen Weintraub whom Trump moved to remove in Feb 2025. The FEC currently has only 2 of 6 commissioners (Broussard and Dara Lindenbaum) and lacks a 4-member policymaking quorum.

records.role: Postmaster General and Chief Executive Officer, United States Postal Service
records.note: 76th Postmaster General. Appointed by the USPS Board of Governors on 2025-05-09; took office 2025-07-15, succeeding Louis DeJoy (resigned March 2025) and acting PMG Doug Tulino.

records.role: Acting Chairman of the Consumer Product Safety Commission
records.note: Feldman (Vice Chairman, elected 2025-01-13) became Acting Chairman on 2025-01-21 when Alex Hoehn-Saric left the chairmanship. Trump removed the three Democratic commissioners (Hoehn-Saric, Mary Boyle, Richard Trumka Jr.) in 2025; the firings were upheld by the U.S. Supreme Court (Trump v. Boyle, July 2025). Still styled 'Acting Chairman' in 2026.

records.role: Chair of the National Transportation Safety Board
records.note: 15th NTSB Chair since August 2021. The Senate reconfirmed her in 2024 for another five-year Board term and a three-year term as Chair; she continues as Chair in 2026.

records.role: Chairman of the Federal Maritime Commission
records.note: Trump designated DiBella as Chairman on 2026-01-28. Predecessor Louis E. Sola, designated chairman 2025-01-20, ended his tenure as Chairman on 2025-06-30.

records.role: Chairman of the Surface Transportation Board
records.note: Trump designated Fuchs as Chairman effective 2025-01-20; reconfirmed to the Board in 2024 with term to 2029-01-14. Continues as Chairman in 2026.

records.role: Chair of the U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
records.note: Designated Chair by unanimous vote of the commissioners in July 2023; commissioner term ends December 2028. Vice Chair is Victoria F. Nourse. Confirmed still Chair on the live USCCR commissioners page in 2026.

records.role: Acting Chairman of the Merit Systems Protection Board
records.note: Kerner (Vice Chairman, confirmed May 2024) became Acting Chairman in February 2025 after Trump removed Chair Cathy Harris; the removal was litigated (reinstated, then re-removed). The Senate confirmed James J. Woodruff II in October 2025, restoring the Board's quorum.

records.role: Chief Justice of the United States
records.jurisdiction: Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States
Supreme Court of the United States

records.role: President pro tempore of the Senate
